title: Score Reconciliation, Waves, Safe Spawns, Pacing, and Difficulty Options · Contract Workshop
summary: A two-person studio incrementally builds fair progression and difficulty model from original geometric fixtures while retaining earlier Python, repository, determinism, frame-budget, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of fair progression and difficulty model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive score from immutable resolved events and reconcile totals by entity tier, streak, bonus, and run.
Advance only when active, queued, and pending-fragment counts satisfy a declared completion invariant.
Select and verify a respawn zone using predicted hazards, toroidal distance, grace time, fallback, and player communication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Score Ledger
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 7665 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Score Ledger.

content.title: Evidence inspection
content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Difficulty Access result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Xbox Accessibility Guidelines 3.2 — Text, contrast, multichannel cues, audio, input, difficulty, focus, timing, motion, photosensitivity, documentation, and testing guidance.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Difficulty Access · changed case · Workshop Difficulty Access, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Difficulty Access precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Difficulty Access · changed case · Workshop Difficulty Access, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A workshop Asteroids team handles case 1903 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Offer descriptive presets and independent assistance for speed, lives, damage, aim, controls, cues, motion, and timing.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Difficulty Access · changed case · Workshop Difficulty Access, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: An easy mode changes game integrity and should be hidden.

stimulus.title: Difficulty Access case evidence
stimulus.caption: Inspect the observation, governing requirement, and mastery evidence before deciding.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: An easy mode changes game integrity and should be hidden.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Offer descriptive presets and independent assistance for speed, lives, damage, aim, controls, cues, motion, and timing.

options.text: For difficulty access · changed case, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Difficulty Access for fair progression and difficulty model.

Offer descriptive presets and independent assistance for speed, lives, damage, aim, controls, cues, motion, and timing. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for fair progression and difficulty model.
An easy mode changes game integrity and should be hidden. That claim fails the difficulty access · changed case evidence boundary for Difficulty Access.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Difficulty Access
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-difficulty-access-1fe2vr" that uses case 8173 to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Difficulty Access.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Difficulty Access into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Difficulty Access, keep Difficulty Access connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-difficulty-access-1fe2vr
# Competency: asteroids-difficulty-access.
# Case 8173: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_difficulty_access_1fe2vr(destroyed: int = 9, score_per_hit: int = 100, score: int = 900, wave: int = 3, safe_spawn: bool = True, difficulty_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1fe2vr = "1fe2vr-rv2ef1-8173"
    assert evidence_variant_1fe2vr
    evidence_axes_1fe2vr = (
        "player1fe2vr",
        "seed8173",
        "inputrv2ef1",
        "frame1fe2vr8173",
        "state81731fe2vr",
        "playtestrv2ef18173",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1fe2vr)) == 6
    if destroyed < 0 or score_per_hit < 0 or wave < 1:
        return False, score, "progression-domain"
    if score != destroyed * score_per_hit:
        return False, score, "score-drift"
    if not safe_spawn:
        return False, wave, "unsafe-spawn"
    if not difficulty_bounded:
        return False, wave, "difficulty-runaway"
    assert score == destroyed * score_per_hit
    return True, wave, "fair-progression"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Difficulty Access structural check is only one gate. fair progression and difficulty model must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Difficulty Access
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 1521 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Offer descriptive presets and independent assistance for speed, lives, damage, aim, controls, cues, motion, and timing.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Offer descriptive presets and independent assistance for speed, lives, damage, aim, controls, cues, motion, and timing.
